preFix.title: Re-felting the bottom of a piece of furniture
preFix.subtitle: Remove old felt pads, clean the feet, and apply new self-adhesive or cut felt to protect floors and reduce noise
preFix.excerpt: How to replace worn or missing felt pads on the feet of chairs, tables, and other furniture. Covers removing old adhesive, cutting felt to size, and applying self-adhesive felt so it stays in place under regular use.

content.text: are the most common cause of scratched wood and tile floors. The job takes ten minutes for a dining set, the only step that can go wrong is fitting new felt over old adhesive residue, which causes the new pad to release within weeks.
Step 1, Remove old pads and adhesive

Tip the furniture upside down or on its side for access to the feet. Peel off any remaining old pads, they usually lift with a fingernail. If a pad is glued solid, slide the utility knife blade flat under the edge and prise it free. Clean the foot surface with white spirit on a cloth to remove all adhesive residue. Allow the white spirit to evaporate before fitting the new felt, adhesive does not bond to a solvent-wet surface.

Pre-cut self-adhesive circles (19 mm, 25 mm, 32 mm diameters) suit most chair and table feet. For large, heavy items or feet wider than 40 mm, cut felt from a roll of 3 mm adhesive-backed sheet, trace the foot outline onto the backing paper, cut just inside the line (oversized felt folds under the foot and peels off), and peel and press. For turned legs with a small curved foot, a 19 mm circle fits without folding. For square legs, cut squares from roll felt so the coverage matches the foot face.

Peel the backing from the felt and press firmly onto the clean, dry foot face. Hold with thumb pressure for 10 seconds. For pieces that get heavy use, dining chairs particularly, apply two overlapping pads rather than one, or use contact cement to bond the felt sheet rather than relying on the factory adhesive alone. Contact cement is spread on both surfaces, left 3 to 5 minutes, and then the felt is pressed into place.

items.fix: Remove the felt, clean with acetone or white spirit, allow to fully dry, and refit. On waxed feet, key the surface lightly with sandpaper before applying the adhesive.
items.cause: Old adhesive residue was not fully removed, or foot surface was contaminated with wax or oil
items.symptom: New felt peels off within days

items.fix: Cut the felt slightly smaller than the foot face so no edge overhangs. Check for grit or debris embedded in the felt surface and replace if dirty.
items.cause: Grit trapped under the felt, or felt overhanging the foot and folding under during movement
items.symptom: Chair still scratches the floor with new felt fitted

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
